title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Test 1
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 2024 - 1999 + 5?
A rectangle has length 8 cm and width 5 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='140' height='80' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='95' y='25' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>8 cm</text><text x='175' y='75' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>5 cm</text></svg>

questions.question_text: Five friends sit in a row. Anna is not at either end. Ben is to the left of Anna. Who could be at the far right end?
questions.solution: Anna cannot be at either end. Ben must be left of Anna, so Ben is not at the far right either. Anyone else can be at the far right.

questions.question_text: What is one half of one quarter of 80?
questions.solution: One quarter of 80 is 20. Half of 20 is 10.

questions.question_text: A triangle has a base of 10 cm and a height of 6 cm. What is its area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,130 190,130 110,30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='110' y1='30' x2='110' y2='130' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><text x='100' y='145' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>10 cm</text><text x='115' y='85' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>6 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Triangle with base 10 cm and height 6 cm.
questions.solution: Area = (1/2)(base)(height) = (1/2)(10)(6) = 30 sq cm.

questions.question_text: Which number is prime?
questions.solution: 29 has no divisors other than 1 and itself. The others factor: 21=3*7, 27=3^3, 33=3*11, 35=5*7.

questions.question_text: A box has 3 red balls, 4 blue balls, and 5 green balls. What is the smallest number of balls you must draw (without looking) to be sure of having at least one ball of each color?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='30' width='240' height='80' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='40' cy='70' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='100' cy='70' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='130' cy='70' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='160' cy='70' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='190' cy='70' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='220' cy='70' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='80' y='25' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>3R + 4B + 5G</text></svg>
questions.solution: Worst case: draw all 5 green and all 4 blue (9 balls), still missing red. The next ball must be red, giving 10.

questions.question_text: A car travels 60 km in 1 hour. How far does it travel in 2.5 hours at the same speed?
questions.solution: Distance = speed x time = 60 x 2.5 = 150 km.

questions.question_text: A square has a side of 7 cm. What is its area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='100' height='100' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='70' y='25' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>7 cm</text></svg>
questions.solution: Area of square = side squared = 7 x 7 = 49 sq cm.

questions.question_text: Tom says: 'I am younger than Lisa.' Lisa says: 'I am younger than Sam.' Who is the oldest?
questions.solution: Tom < Lisa < Sam, so Sam is the oldest.

questions.question_text: The figure shows two squares. The larger has side 10 and the smaller has side 6. What is the area of the shaded (non-overlapping) region?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='120' height='120' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='140' y='80' width='60' height='60' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='65' y='160' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>10</text><text x='160' y='160' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>6</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two separate squares of sides 10 and 6.
questions.solution: The squares are separate. Total area = 10*10 + 6*6 = 100 + 36 = 136.

questions.question_text: In a tournament, each of 5 teams plays every other team exactly once. How many games are played in total?
questions.solution: Number of games = C(5,2) = (5 x 4)/2 = 10.

questions.question_text: Anna has twice as many stickers as Ben. Together they have 36. How many does Anna have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='60' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='35' y='60' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>Ben</text><rect x='100' y='40' width='120' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='140' y='60' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>Anna (2x)</text><text x='80' y='110' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>Total = 36</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Bar diagram showing Anna has twice as many as Ben.
questions.solution: Let Ben have x; Anna has 2x. 3x = 36, x = 12. Anna has 24.

questions.question_text: If 3/5 of a number is 24, what is the number?
A right triangle has legs 5 and 12. What is its hypotenuse?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,150 170,150 30,30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='90' y='165' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>12</text><text x='10' y='95' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>5</text><text x='105' y='85' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.solution: By the Pythagorean theorem: 5^2 + 12^2 = 25 + 144 = 169, so hypotenuse = 13.

questions.question_text: Four children A, B, C, D finish a race. A is not first. B is not last. C is just before D. Which of the following orderings is possible?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 280 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='30' width='60' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='80' y='30' width='60' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='150' y='30' width='60' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='220' y='30' width='60' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='30' y='55' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>1st</text><text x='100' y='55' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>2nd</text><text x='170' y='55' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>3rd</text><text x='240' y='55' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>4th</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four ranking slots for a race.
questions.solution: Check B,C,D,A: A not first (ok), B not last (ok), C just before D (ok). All constraints satisfied.

questions.question_text: What is the greatest common divisor of 48 and 60?
A train leaves at 9:15 and arrives at 11:50. How long is the journey in minutes?
questions.solution: From 9:15 to 11:15 is 120 minutes; from 11:15 to 11:50 is 35 more. Total 155 minutes.

questions.question_text: A cube has an edge of 4 cm. What is its surface area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,60 120,60 120,140 40,140' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='40,60 70,30 150,30 120,60' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='120,60 150,30 150,110 120,140' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='70' y='160' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.solution: Surface area = 6 x side^2 = 6 x 16 = 96 sq cm.

questions.question_text: How many different three-digit numbers can be formed using the digits 1, 2, 3, 4 if no digit is repeated?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='30' width='50' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='90' y='30' width='50' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='160' y='30' width='50' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='38' y='60' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>4</text><text x='108' y='60' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>3</text><text x='178' y='60' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>2</text></svg>
questions.solution: 4 choices for the first digit, 3 for the second, 2 for the third: 4 x 3 x 2 = 24.

questions.question_text: Solve for x: 3(x - 4) = 2x + 5.
In the figure, ABCD is a square of side 8. M is the midpoint of BC. What is the area of triangle ABM?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='140' height='140' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='30,30 170,30 170,100' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='20' y='25' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>A</text><text x='175' y='25' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>B</text><text x='175' y='185' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>C</text><text x='20' y='185' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>D</text><text x='180' y='105' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>M</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Square ABCD with M midpoint of BC. Triangle ABM shaded.
questions.solution: Triangle ABM has base AB = 8 and height BM = 4. Area = (1/2)(8)(4) = 16.

questions.question_text: Four statements are written. Exactly one is true. (1) Statement 2 is true. (2) Statement 3 is false. (3) Statement 4 is true. (4) 2+2=5. Which statement is true?
questions.solution: Statement 4 is false. If statement 2 is true then statement 3 is false, meaning statement 4 is not true, consistent. Statements 1 and 3 false as well. Only statement 2 is true.

questions.question_text: A worker paints a fence in 6 hours. Another paints the same fence in 3 hours. Working together, how many hours do they need?
questions.solution: Combined rate = 1/6 + 1/3 = 1/2 fence per hour. Time = 1 / (1/2) = 2 hours.

questions.question_text: What is the remainder when 7^10 is divided by 5?
A circle has a radius of 5. A square is inscribed in it (vertices on the circle). What is the area of the square?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='100' cy='100' r='70' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='100,30 170,100 100,170 30,100' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='105' y='100' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>5</text><line x1='100' y1='100' x2='170' y2='100' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Square inscribed in circle of radius 5.
questions.solution: The diagonal of the square equals the diameter = 10. Area = diagonal^2 / 2 = 100/2 = 50.

questions.question_text: You have a 3-liter jug and a 5-liter jug. What is the fewest pours needed to measure exactly 4 liters in the 5-liter jug, starting empty?
questions.solution: Fill 5, pour into 3 (leaves 2 in 5), empty 3, pour 2 from 5 into 3, fill 5, pour from 5 into 3 until full (1 liter poured). 5 now has 4. That is 6 pours/operations.

questions.question_text: If 2^x = 32, what is x?
In how many ways can 3 identical red balls be placed into 4 distinct boxes (a box may hold several or none)?
questions.solution: Stars and bars: C(3 + 4 - 1, 3) = C(6,3) = 20.
